mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159539 thousand as of Jan 2014
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% annualized growth, real rates are restrictive and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 322.132 as of Jan 2014
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising month-over-month by more than 5% signals accelerating residential investment and forward-looking demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1321.0 thousand as of Dec 2013
failure_condition: Positive HOUST surprises do not produce significantly higher 5-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling demand destruction and supply-side weakness that reduces corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.9867 and UNRATE at 4.2% as of Jan 2014
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO and UNRATE deterioration is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain flat or declining, indicating consumer demand weakness that portends earnings misses and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 2% AND HOUST month-over-month change ≤ 0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 726283.0 million and HOUST at 1321.0 thousand as of Dec 2013
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the correlation between RSAFS and HOUST weakness and subsequent SPX returns is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) turns negative month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 159539 thousand as of Jan 2014
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint deterioration signal does not predict negative returns significantly better than either series alone (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 4.0% signals monetary easing that compresses equity discount rates and increases asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2% annualized while remaining above its 12-month moving average, signaling sustained supply-side strength and capacity utilization that supports corporate margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND INDPRO > 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum does not produce significantly higher 5-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.
